title: Admin/Operator Tenant Control Plane
decision_rationale: Existing operator model (PBKDF2 hashing, fail-closed auth, safe to_dict) was already hardened for controlled preview. GL-206 adds explicit tenant assignment, admin-only control-plane HTTP routes, revoke function, and structured audit events. create_operator() now requires explicit tenant_id (silent default removed; all existing callers already passed it explicitly). Revoked operators fail closed. Safe response fields enforced in list/read routes. Structured log events emitted for operator_created and operator_revoked (no raw token/hash in events). Remaining gaps (production multi-tenant isolation, OAuth/JWT, workspace enforcement, OpenAPI update) are documented. No production SaaS claim. GrantLayer remains Developer Preview / Controlled Preview.
context: GL-200A through GL-205 are merged. GL-204 identified admin-plane tenant isolation (GL-200D, RB-004) as P0 blocker. GL-206 hardens operator.py, adds admin control-plane routes in server.py, and documents the admin/operator model semantics.
scope: operators.py hardening, admin HTTP control-plane routes, revoke operator, safe response fields, tenant assignment enforcement, structured audit log events, documentation
non_goals: Public publish, GitHub push, or visibility change
Weakening of GL-200 through GL-205 baselines
OpenAPI contract update (admin routes are internal, additive)
current_admin_operator_model_summary: Admin uses GRANTLAYER_ADMIN_TOKEN Bearer token with hmac.compare_digest (constant-time). Admin mode binds to 'demo' tenant. Operators use PBKDF2-SHA256 (600k iterations) Bearer token with SHA-256 lookup hash for narrowing. Revoked/inactive operators (active=0) fail closed at DB query level. Operator to_dict() never exposes token_hash, lookup_hash, or raw token. Before GL-206: create_operator() had silent 'dev' tenant_id default; no admin HTTP routes for operator CRUD; no revoke function.
tenant_assignment_model: tenant_id is set at operator creation time by the admin. create_operator() now requires explicit tenant_id (positional argument, no default). The operator cannot override their own tenant_id at authentication — it is read from the DB row, not from any request header. Admin provides tenant_id in POST /admin/operators request body. Bootstrap operator uses tenant_id='demo' explicitly.
admin_behavior_model: Admin authenticates via GRANTLAYER_ADMIN_TOKEN Bearer token. Fail-closed: missing token=401, invalid token=403, REQUIRE_ADMIN_TOKEN=false in production=startup error. Admin can create/list/read/revoke operators via /admin/operators routes. Admin can access resources as 'demo' tenant via legacy admin-token mode. Admin cannot access operator routes with operator token (403).
operator_behavior_model: Operators authenticate via PBKDF2-hashed Bearer token. On auth failure: 401 with reason code. Tenant context is server-derived from DB operator row. Operator can access resources within their assigned tenant only. Operator cannot access /admin/operators routes (403). Operator cannot modify own tenant_id. Operator cannot escalate to admin role. Revoked operators (active=0) fail closed at auth query.
control_plane_api_behavior: POST /admin/operators: requires admin token + {name, role, tenantId}; returns safe fields + one-time raw token on 201. GET /admin/operators: admin-only list, safe fields only. GET /admin/operators/{id}: admin-only read, safe fields only, 404 if not found. POST /admin/operators/{id}/revoke: admin-only revoke, sets active=0, returns {ok, operatorId, revoked}. All routes: no token_hash, lookup_hash, or raw token in list/read responses.
audit_behavior: Structured log events emitted via safe_log() using operator_action event type (already defined in structured_logging.py). operator_created event includes operator_id and tenant_id (no raw token, no hash). operator_revoked event includes operator_id (no raw token, no hash). The audit_events hash-chain is NOT used for control-plane events — it is reserved for grant/access audit immutability. Hash-chain is preserved unchanged.
security_model: Fail-closed at every layer: missing/invalid admin token=401/403; operator on admin route=403; revoked operator=401; missing tenantId on create=400. Token safety: PBKDF2 hash for storage, SHA-256 for lookup, raw token returned once on create only. Tenant isolation: server-derived tenant context, no client header override. Cross-tenant denial enforced at all resource endpoints (GL-200A/B/C). Operator cannot self-escalate.
production_mode_implications: Admin control-plane routes are suitable for controlled preview with synthetic/demo data. Not suitable for production multi-tenant without full GL-200D closure. No OAuth/JWT/SSO. No workspace_id enforcement. No production IAM. These remain P0 blockers for production SaaS.
controlled_preview_impact: GL-206 is compatible with GL-198 controlled preview boundary. Admin can create operators per synthetic/demo tenant. Operators remain scoped to assigned tenant. No real customer data. Admin control-plane routes are additive and do not weaken GL-200 through GL-205 guarantees.
implementation_summary: backend/src/operators.py: create_operator() tenant_id now required positional arg; revoke_operator() added; _operator_to_safe_dict() added; list_operators_for_admin() added; get_operator_safe() added. backend/src/server.py: import secrets added; path normalization for admin routes; GET /admin/operators (list); GET /admin/operators/{id} (read); POST /admin/operators (create with explicit tenantId); POST /admin/operators/{id}/revoke (revoke). auth.py, audit_log.py, models.py, migrations: unchanged.
remaining_gaps: GL-200D full closure: admin token binds to demo tenant only; full multi-tenant operator isolation requires per-tenant admin hierarchy
No operator token rotation endpoint (manual: create new + revoke old)
No OAuth/JWT/SSO (PBKDF2/Bearer for controlled preview only)
No production IAM (deferred to future issues)
OpenAPI docs/openapi.yaml not updated with admin routes (internal-only, additive)
Stale claims in README/AGENTS.md (deferred per GL-204)
No TLS, container hardening, or orchestration
No live PostgreSQL validation executed (P0 from GL-205)
No automated backup or DR runbooks (P0 from GL-205)

risk_register.risk: Admin token leaked gives full operator management access
risk_register.mitigation: Keep admin token in environment variable; rotate on suspicion; fail-closed

risk_register.risk: Operator created with wrong tenant_id cannot be corrected without revoke+recreate
risk_register.mitigation: Admin can revoke and recreate; document in runbook

risk_register.risk: Cross-tenant operator access via admin in multi-tenant production
risk_register.mitigation: Do not deploy multi-tenant production without GL-200D full closure

risk_register.risk: One-time token on create not captured by caller
risk_register.mitigation: Token returned once on 201; caller must store immediately; revoke and recreate if lost

risk_register.risk: Revoked operator re-authenticates if DB has connection issues
risk_register.mitigation: Fail-closed: auth query requires active=1; connection failure causes auth failure

recommended_next_issues: GL-207 Claim Safety & Controlled Preview Boundary
